items.hook: Trinity Western University (TWU) is a Christian liberal arts college and research university with campuses in Langley and Richmond, British Columbia, Canada.
items.body: TWU is a research institution offering undergraduate and graduate degrees in the humanities and sciences as well as in several professional schools. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: A diplomatic conference adopts the Rome Statute of the International Criminal Court, establishing the permanent international court in The Hague, to prosecute individuals for genocide, crimes against humanity, war crimes, and the crime of aggression.
items.body: The Rome Statute of the International Criminal Court is the treaty that established the International Criminal Court (ICC). The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Movistar Plus will support 26 Spanish series a year - Production / Funding - Spain
items.body: The Spanish platform is expanding its investment and commitment to drama by doubling the number of titles and introducing new collaboration models

items.hook: Davenport College is one of the fourteen residential colleges of Yale University.
items.body: Its buildings were completed in 1933 mainly in the Georgian style but with a gothic façade along York Street. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Toronto adds special presentations including ‘Alpha Gang’, ‘Love Of Your Life’
items.body: Other world premieres joining the roster include Ben Shirinian’s ‘The Housewife’.

items.hook: In the field of artificial intelligence the study of naïve physics is a part of the effort to formalize the common knowledge of human beings.
items.body: Naïve physics or folk physics is the untrained human perception of basic physical phenomena. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Mathieu Amalric's Autobiography of an Octopus now in post-production - Production / Funding - France/Belgium
items.body: Christèle Tual toplines the filmmaker’s new feature, produced by Les Films du Poisson and sold by Pyramide International, which is an adaptation of Vinciane Despret’s novel

items.hook: At least 120 people are killed and 130 injured by a suicide bombing in Diyala Governorate, Iraq.
items.body: A suicide car bombing occurred on 17 July 2015 in the Iraqi city of Khan Bani Saad, targeting a local marketplace. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Interview: Nader Saeivar • Director of Hijamat - “I wasn’t interested in judging, I wanted to observe how mechanisms continue to shape people’s lives” - Karlovy Vary 2026 - Competition
items.body: The filmmaker discusses his latest film, centred on a traditional Muslim family and community whose world is shaken when they discover that one of their young members is gay

items.hook: The Sune series is a series of books for children and young adults, published since 1984, by Swedish writers Anders Jacobsson and Sören Olsson.
items.body: The stories were originally 90 episodes made for the Swedish Radio and broadcast in 1983–93. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Members of the French National Guard under the command of General Lafayette open fire on a crowd of radical Jacobins at the Champ de Mars, Paris, during the French Revolution, killing scores of people.
items.body: The National Guard is a French military, gendarmerie, and police reserve force, active in its current form since 2016 but originally founded in 1789 during the French Revolution. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: National Year of Reading should extend to a decade, inquiry says
items.body:

items.hook: NEC Corporation is organized as the first Japanese joint venture with foreign capital.
items.body: NEC Corporation is a Japanese multinational information technology corporation headquartered at the NEC Supertower in Minato, Tokyo, Japan. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Venice Immersive selects 68 Extended Reality projects - Venice 2026
items.body: From 2 to 12 September, the jury comprising Paul Raphaël, Négar Motevalymeidanshah and Kate Voet will assess projects from 26 countries

items.hook: Fantasia shines a spotlight on European genre cinema with a wealth of premieres - Fantasia 2026
items.body: The new films by Agnieszka Smoczyńska and Kasper Kalle lead the European contingent at the Canadian festival

items.hook: After takeoff from Husein Sastranegara International Airport, Sempati Air Flight 304 crashes into a residential neighborhood in Bandung, killing 28 people.
items.body: Husein Sastranegara Airport is a domestic airport serving Bandung, the capital of West Java, Indonesia. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: First Indochina War: Viet Minh troops successfully ambush the armoured French column 'G.M. 42' in the Battle of Chu Dreh Pass in the Central Highlands. It is the last battle of the war.
items.body: The First Indochina War, known alternatively internationally as the French Indochina War, was fought in French Indochina between France and the Viet Minh and their respective allies, from 19 December 1946 until 11 August 1954. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: The history of slavery in California began with the enslavement of Indigenous Californians under Spanish colonial rule.
items.body: The arrival of the Spanish colonists introduced chattel slavery and involuntary servitude to the area. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Penicillium commune is an indoor fungus belonging to the genus Penicillium.
items.body: It is known as one of the most common fungi spoilage moulds on cheese. The world is stranger than it lets on.
